chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P.NO.S-1219 of 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For orders on MA-2570/2017 3. For katcha peshi. 29.09.2017 Mr. Asim Shabbir Soomro, Advocate a/w petitioner. Mr. Imtiaz Ali Mahar, State Counsel. = On the last date of hearing, the respondents No.2 and 3 filed their reply/written reply in the case, wherein it has been stated that the controversy between the parties i.e. petitioner and the private respondents No.4 to 9 is a dispute of civil nature and the official respondents have no role to play in this matter. It has also been stated that Criminal Miscellaneous Application under Section 133 Cr.P.C filed by the petitioner against the private respondents is also pending adjudication before the Court of concerned Magistrate. It is also stated that the petitioner never approached the official respondents for redressal of their grievances mentioned in the petition. Further stated that in the event the petitioner approaches to the concerned police station for legal protection they will provide protection according to law. Be that as it may, this petition is being disposed of with the direction to the private respondents No.4 to 9, namely Baboo, Muhammad Moosa, Ahsan Memon, Habibullah, Inayatullah and Aijaz not to cause any harassment to the petitioner with further direction to the official respondents to discharge their functions and duties strictly in accordance with law in a fair and reasonable manner without patronizing any group or individuals. The petitioner and the private respondents are at liberty to seek remedy of their grievances,

Be that as it may, this petition is being disposed of with the direction to the private respondents No.4 to 9, namely Baboo, Muhammad Moosa, Ahsan Memon, Habibullah, Inayatullah and Aijaz not to cause any harassment to the petitioner with further direction to the official respondents to discharge their functions and duties strictly in accordance with law in a fair and reasonable manner without patronizing any group or individuals. The petitioner and the private respondents are at liberty to seek remedy of their grievances, if any, before the appropriate forum by invoking due process of law and not by taking law in their hands. Since the private respondents No.4 to 9 despite notice are not in attendance, therefore, the concerned SHO is also directed to serve the copy of this order on the above named private respondents by calling them in Police Station and compliance of such service should be furnished with the Additional Registrar of this Court. JUDGE Shahid
